2605.01382.title: Sparse Representation Learning for Vessels
2605.01382.paper_summary: Analyzing human vasculature and vessel-like, tubular structures, such as airways, is crucial for disease diagnosis and treatment. Current methods often rely on small sub-regions or simplified tree-like structures, rendering analysis of entire organ-level networks at clinical resolution computationally challenging. To this end, we propose VAEsselSparse, an efficient encoder-decoder model to obtain a meaningful yet compact representation of the entire organ-level vascular network at sub-millimeter resolution. VAEsselSparse leverages the inherent sparsity of 3D vascular structures via sparse convolutions and attention mechanisms, achieving substantial spatial compression rates of 8 x 8 x 8. We demonstrate superior reconstruction performance compared to dense counterparts and previous methods. Importantly, the resulting latent space retains clinically relevant discriminative features readily usable for classification tasks, such as aneurysm/stenosis or subvariants of the circle of Willis. Moreover, the compact latent space of VAEsselSparse serves as an effective representation for learning vessel-specific priors through generative models, enabling the synthesis of realistic vasculature.
2605.01382.paper_summary_zh: 分析人類血管系統和類血管的管狀結構（如氣道）對於疾病診斷和治療至關重要。當前的方法通常依賴於小的子區域或簡化的樹狀結構，這使得在臨床分辨率下分析整個器官級網絡在計算上具有挑戰性。為此，我們提出了VAEsselSparse，一種高效的編碼器-解碼器模型，用於獲取整個器官級血管網絡在亞毫米分辨率下的有意義且緊湊的表示。VAEsselSparse通過稀疏卷積和注意機制利用3D血管結構的固有稀疏性，實現了8 x 8 x 8的顯著空間壓縮率。我們展示了與密集對應物和先前方法相比，重建性能的優越性。重要的是，生成的潛在空間保留了臨床相關的區別特徵，這些特徵可直接用於分類任務，如動脈瘤/狹窄或威利斯圈的亞變種。此外，VAEsselSparse的緊湊潛在空間作為學習血管特定先驗的有效表示，通過生成模型使得合成現實的血管結構成為可能。
2605.01382.author: Chinmay Prabhakar et.al.
2605.01382.authors: Chinmay Prabhakar, Bastian Wittmann, Paul Büschl, Hongwei Bran Li, Bjoern Menze, Suprosanna Shit
